metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief rationale in the Description explaining why this new adapter uses lizard as an imported library rather than shelling it out (as the existing backfill script and grounding engine do), and note that this deliberately supersedes/complements the CLI pattern documented in pyproject.toml.
evidence: pyproject.toml line 142: 'The out-of-process backends (universal-ctags, OpenGrep/ast-grep, scc/lizard) are external CLIs, not pip deps.'
scripts/backfill_complexity_clone_snapshots.py:50: subprocess.run(['lizard', tree], ...) — existing working CLI usage
src/rebar/grounding/engine_b.py:60: _METRIC_CANDIDATES = ('scc', 'lizard') — lizard treated as binary on PATH
Plan Description: 'lizard is used AS AN IMPORTED LIBRARY here (via guard_import(extra="metrics"))' — no justification for the switch
findings.finding: The plan pivots lizard from the established CLI/subprocess pattern (used in backfill_complexity_clone_snapshots.py and the grounding engine's _METRIC_CANDIDATES) to an in-process library import without justifying why — directly contradicting the pyproject.toml's explicit comment that classifies lizard as an 'external CLI, not pip dep'. The prior architectural decision was intentional; this ticket reverses it silently.
findings.location: Description ('lizard is used AS AN IMPORTED LIBRARY here') vs. pyproject.toml line 142 comment and scripts/backfill_complexity_clone_snapshots.py subprocess usage
scenarios: An implementer reading the existing code and pyproject.toml comment will see an explicit statement that lizard is a CLI, not a pip dep — creating confusion about which architecture to follow.
If both patterns coexist (CLI in backfill/grounding, library in analyzers), the codebase will have two incompatible models for lizard usage with no documented rationale for the split.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Description: e.g., 'The library API is preferred over subprocess here because it avoids PATH dependency, is mockable in-process for unit tests, and provides structured per-function data directly (no output parsing needed); the grounding engine and backfill script will continue using the CLI path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC1 that the scan path is passed as an argument to the `Analyzer.analyze(path)` call (from 3b30's protocol), not pulled from the e7a0 config at import/init time, so the adapter is self-contained and testable without 0734.
evidence: e7a0 description: 'SCOPE IS SCHEMA-ONLY — this ticket does not change any metric runtime behavior and does not remove any hardcode; consuming the config + the Unavailable-on-unconfigured behavior land in migration 0734'
Plan says 'deps are sibling stories' — but e7a0 only ships the schema, not the runtime wiring
findings.finding: The plan states the adapter 'runs lizard over the scan root from e7a0', but e7a0 is explicitly schema-only ('SCOPE IS SCHEMA-ONLY — this ticket does not change any metric runtime behavior'); runtime consumption of scan_roots lands in ticket 0734. The plan does not clarify how the adapter receives its scan path at call-time — leaving it ambiguous whether the path is passed via the Analyzer protocol (call-time argument) or is expected to be read from config (which is not yet wired).
findings.location: Description: 'the scan root from e7a0' / Acceptance criteria AC1: 'runs lizard over the scan root'
scenarios: Implementer attempts to call config.code_health.scan_roots at init time -> KeyError or empty list because 0734 hasn't wired it yet
Implementer correctly passes path via Analyzer.analyze(path) argument but plan gives no guidance, causing inconsistency with what AC1 actually means

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State an explicit default for token/param counts in AC1 (e.g. 'not collected by default; CCN is the required output') so the criterion is unambiguous and measurable.
evidence: 'optionally token/param counts' — no default stated, no condition given for when they should be included
Verification section has no mention of token/param counts, suggesting they may be entirely out of scope yet AC1 leaves the door open
findings.finding: AC1 uses 'optionally token/param counts' without stating a default, deferring the design decision to the executor and making the criterion partially unmeasurable (it is unclear whether a run that omits token/param counts satisfies AC1 or not).
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC1: 'per-file/aggregate CCN (optionally token/param counts)'
scenarios: Executor implements token/param count population and a reviewer rejects it as out-of-scope
Executor omits token/param counts and a reviewer flags AC1 as incomplete
Conflicting interpretations cause rework at review time
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC1 to: 'runs lizard over the scan root → per-file/aggregate CCN populated in `AnalyzerResult.complexity`; token count and parameter count are NOT collected (out of scope for this story).' — or explicitly include them if desired.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the decision to use lizard as the CCN backend (and the optional token/param-count scope) in an ADR, AGENTS.md entry, or equivalent design note.
evidence: 'Live in src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/' — new pipeline stage
No mention of ADR, AGENTS.md update, or design-doc entry
This establishes lizard as the project's CCN strategy across ~25 languages — a non-trivial architectural decision
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new analyzer pipeline stage (lizard_complexity.py wired into the metrics system via the AnalyzerResult interface) with no ADR or AGENTS.md/design-doc update capturing the architectural decision to use lizard as the CCN backend.
scenarios: A future contributor evaluating whether to replace or extend the CCN approach has no recorded rationale for the lizard choice
The 'optionally token/param counts' scope question is unresolved and undocumented, leaving future maintainers uncertain about intended extension points

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific lizard Python API entry point(s) and result-object field(s) used (e.g., `lizard.analyze_file(path)` -> `.function_list[i].cyclomatic_complexity`) — either in AC1 or in an implementation note — so the library surface is locked before coding begins.
evidence: Existing codebase uses lizard exclusively as CLI: `subprocess.run(["lizard", tree], ...)` in scripts/backfill_complexity_clone_snapshots.py:50
Plan states: 'lizard is used AS AN IMPORTED LIBRARY here (via guard_import(extra="metrics"))'
Spike evidence (spike2_derisk.py:49-55) only checks `import lizard` importability, not the Python API surface (no call to analyze_file/analyze_files, no inspection of result object fields)
resolve_symbol for lizard.analyze_file and lizard.analyze_files both returned UNRESOLVED — library not installed in review environment
Evidence class: Partially-verified (library importable; specific method/attribute names unverified)
findings.finding: The plan integrates lizard via its Python library API (new surface — existing codebase only shells it via subprocess), but names no specific API entry points (e.g., `lizard.analyze_file()`, `lizard.analyze_files()`) or result object attributes (e.g., the field holding CCN per function). The spike (spike2_derisk.py) only verified `import lizard` succeeds and that lizard takes files; it did NOT probe the library API shape. AC1 ('runs lizard over the scan root -> per-file/aggregate CCN') is therefore unverified at the method/attribute level.
findings.location: AC1: 'analyzers/lizard_complexity.py runs lizard over the scan root -> per-file/aggregate CCN (optionally token/param counts)'
scenarios: Implementor picks the wrong method name (e.g., iterates CLI stdout instead of using analyze_file()) and the adapter silently returns empty results or crashes at runtime when lizard is installed.
The result object field for CCN differs from the assumed name (e.g., `.cyclomatic_complexity` vs `.CCN`) causing a silent zero or AttributeError when the adapter runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1 or a new 'Implementation notes' section: 'Call `lizard.analyze_file(path)` (or `lizard.analyze_files([...])`) for each file under the scan root; read `.cyclomatic_complexity` (and optionally `.token_count`, `.parameter_count`) from each `FunctionInfo` in `.function_list`.' Verify these names against `lizard.__version__` ≥ 1.23 before committing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the two dependency lists: either add 3b30 to 'Recorded edges' or explain why it is excluded (e.g., 'interface already merged').
evidence: 'Dependencies: 3b30, 9597, e7a0 (siblings)' lists three deps
'Recorded edges: depends_on 9597, e7a0' lists only two, silently dropping 3b30
No rationale given for the omission
findings.finding: Dependency 3b30 is named in the prose Dependencies line but intentionally omitted from 'Recorded edges' without any explanation, creating two inconsistent dependency lists that could confuse dependency-tracking tooling or a reviewer checking graph completeness.
findings.location: Dependencies section: 'Dependencies: 3b30, 9597, e7a0 (siblings). Recorded edges: depends_on 9597, e7a0.'
scenarios: A dependency-graph tool reads only 'Recorded edges' and misses the 3b30 interface dependency, allowing the story to be scheduled before 3b30 is merged.
A reviewer auditing completeness finds two inconsistent lists and cannot determine which is authoritative.
findings.suggested_fix: Add 3b30 to the recorded edges: 'Recorded edges: depends_on 3b30, 9597, e7a0' — or add a parenthetical such as '(3b30 interface already landed; no edge needed)' to make the omission intentional and traceable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `accruing_since` value (e.g., a fixed ISO-8601 string literal matching the story's inception date) to use when constructing `Unavailable` on the missing-extra path in `lizard_complexity.py`.
evidence: registry.py: `@dataclass(frozen=True) class Unavailable: reason: str; accruing_since: str` — both fields required, no default
Plan never names a source for the accruing_since value (hardcoded timestamp? constructor argument? config-derived?)
3b30 review note: 'the fallback helper returns Unavailable(reason=..., accruing_since=<ts>)' — confirms ts is required but source unspecified here
findings.finding: The plan specifies returning `Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` on missing extra, but never states what value to supply for the required `accruing_since` field. The `Unavailable` dataclass in `registry.py` has two mandatory fields (`reason: str`, `accruing_since: str`), so the implementation cannot be completed without this specification.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria: 'Extra not installed -> OptionalDependencyError caught -> Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)'
scenarios: Implementer omits accruing_since -> TypeError at runtime when metrics extra is absent
Implementer guesses wrong value (e.g. datetime.now()) -> inconsistent sentinel shape diverges from how other Unavailable sentinels are stamped
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Acceptance Criteria: 'Unavailable returned on missing extra uses a fixed `accruing_since` ISO-8601 timestamp (e.g. "2026-07-18T00:00:00+00:00") consistent with other code_health metrics in REGISTRY.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Wrap lizard analysis calls in a try/except and return `Unavailable` (or a partial result with a warning) for runtime errors, not just missing-dependency errors.
evidence: AC only specifies: 'Extra not installed -> OptionalDependencyError caught -> Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)'
No mention of try/except around lizard.analyze_files() or per-file error handling for runtime failures
lizard can raise on malformed/binary input or unsupported edge-cases
findings.finding: Runtime errors from lizard (e.g., corrupt file, encoding error, unexpected exception during analysis) are not handled — only the missing-dependency path is specified. A crash in lizard mid-scan will propagate as an unhandled exception rather than a graceful Unavailable or partial-result return.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — 'analyzers/lizard_complexity.py runs lizard over the scan root'
scenarios: Scan root contains a binary file or file with unusual encoding that lizard cannot parse, causing an unhandled exception
lizard encounters an internal error on a valid but edge-case source file
Partial scan completes for some files but fails on others with no partial result returned
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the `lizard.analyze_files(...)` call in a broad `except Exception as e:` block that logs the error and returns `Unavailable(reason=str(e), accruing_since=...)`, mirroring the pattern already used for OptionalDependencyError.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least a structured log statement (e.g., `logger.warning(...)`) when the analyzer returns `Unavailable` or catches a runtime error, so failures are observable in production logs.
evidence: No mention of logging, metrics emission, or tracing anywhere in the plan
'Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)' is the specified return but no instrumentation of that path is called out
findings.finding: No observability is specified for new failure points: there is no logging, metric, or trace mentioned for when the lizard analyzer fails, returns Unavailable, or produces partial results.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria / Files likely touched: lizard_complexity.py
scenarios: Lizard analyzer silently returns Unavailable in production; operators have no visibility into why complexity metrics are absent
A regression in lizard or the scan root causes the analyzer to silently degrade with no alert

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one test for each missing failure/boundary path: (a) empty scan root → well-defined result (empty list or Unavailable), (b) scan root containing an unparseable/binary file → adapter handles gracefully without raising, (c) lizard internal error → adapter returns Unavailable or raises a typed error rather than leaking a raw exception.
evidence: Verification says only: 'Unit over Python + one other language fixture; guard-absent -> Unavailable'
No mention of empty scan root, unparseable/binary file, or zero-function file behavior
AC line 1 says 'optionally token/param counts' but nothing about what the adapter returns when lizard raises internally
findings.finding: The testing plan covers the happy path (CCN populated for Python + one other language) and the import-guard path, but does not require any test for lizard-runtime failure modes: e.g., an empty scan root (no files), a file lizard cannot parse (binary or syntax-error file), or a scan root that yields zero functions. These are realistic inputs to the adapter and constitute missing failure/boundary paths for a new user-facing flow.
findings.location: Verification section; AC line 'Unit over Python + one other language fixture; guard-absent -> Unavailable'
scenarios: Caller passes a scan root with only binary files; lizard raises or returns nothing; adapter behavior is undefined and untested.
Scan root is empty (no files); adapter may raise or return an empty/zero aggregate; not specified or tested.
Lizard encounters a file with a severe syntax error and raises an exception internally; adapter swallows or propagates it; not tested.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Verification section: 'edge-cases: empty scan root returns empty/zero aggregate without raising; a binary or unparseable file in the root is skipped or returns Unavailable rather than propagating a raw lizard exception.' Create corresponding fixture files (e.g., a zero-byte file and a file with intentional syntax errors) in tests/unit/metrics/fixtures/.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to the Verification section (e.g. `pytest tests/unit/metrics/test_lizard_complexity.py -v`) so every AC has a copy-pasteable command that produces pass/fail evidence.
evidence: No `pytest` invocation path, no assertion target, no fixture file names are given in the Verification section
'Files likely touched' names the test file (`tests/unit/metrics/test_lizard_complexity.py`) but the Verification section does not reference it with a runnable command
findings.finding: The Verification section contains no concrete proving command, only a prose description; an executing agent cannot derive a deterministic, copy-pasteable command that produces pass/fail evidence for each AC.
findings.location: Verification section: 'Unit over Python + one other language fixture; guard-absent -> Unavailable.'
scenarios: An automated agent executing the plan cannot determine which command to run to certify completion
A human reviewer cannot run a single copy-pasted command to confirm all three ACs are green
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Verification: `pytest tests/unit/metrics/test_lizard_complexity.py -v` — expected: all tests pass, including the guard-absent case that returns `Unavailable`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly gate this ticket on 9597 being merged first (or add a hard `depends_on 9597` ordering note), and confirm that `'metrics'` appears in `_optional.EXTRAS` before `guard_import(extra='metrics')` is called — otherwise the guard raises `ValueError` instead of `OptionalDependencyError`.
evidence: src/rebar/_optional.py line 73: `if extra not in EXTRAS: raise ValueError(f'unknown extra {extra!r} ...')`
src/rebar/_optional.py lines 32–45: EXTRAS dict contains 'agents', 'eval', 'tracing', 'grounding' — 'metrics' is absent
9597 ticket status: open (not yet delivered); 9597 is the ticket that adds 'metrics' to EXTRAS
Plan states 'deps are sibling stories' but does not specify that 9597 must be merged before this ticket's guard will produce OptionalDependencyError rather than ValueError
findings.finding: `guard_import(extra="metrics")` will raise `ValueError` (not `OptionalDependencyError`) until the `"metrics"` key is registered in `_optional.EXTRAS` — which is delivered by the sibling ticket 9597, currently open. The plan asserts the guard mechanism works but does not require 9597 to be merged first, leaving a window where the guard silently misfires with the wrong exception type.
findings.location: Review-driven corrections / AC line 2: 'Extra not installed -> `OptionalDependencyError` caught -> Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)'
scenarios: Developer implements this ticket calling `guard_import('lizard', extra='metrics')` before 9597 is merged — gets `ValueError: unknown extra 'metrics'` instead of `OptionalDependencyError`, breaking the Unavailable fallback contract.
CI runs tests for this ticket in an environment where 9597 hasn't landed — the 'guard-absent -> Unavailable' verification test would fail with ValueError, not a caught OptionalDependencyError.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `9597` to the formal `depends_on` edges (it is listed in Dependencies text but only `e7a0` and `9597` are listed as recorded edges — verify the edge is recorded). In the implementation, add a note or assertion: `assert 'metrics' in EXTRAS` after the guard_import call site, or simply ensure CI merges 9597 before this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/__init__.py` (new, even if empty) to 'Files likely touched' — the `analyzers/` directory is an entirely new Python package and requires an `__init__.py` to be importable.
evidence: Plan lists only `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/lizard_complexity.py (new)` and the test file.
Confirmed via list_directory that `src/rebar/metrics/` has no `analyzers/` subdirectory — entire package is net-new.
Sibling `src/rebar/metrics/adapters/` demonstrates the pattern: `__init__.py` must exist (confirmed at `src/rebar/metrics/adapters/__init__.py`).
findings.finding: The plan's 'Files likely touched' omits `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/__init__.py`. Creating `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/` as a new Python package requires an `__init__.py` to be importable; without it, `from rebar.metrics.analyzers.lizard_complexity import ...` will fail with a ModuleNotFoundError.
scenarios: Implementation creates `lizard_complexity.py` but omits `__init__.py`, making `import rebar.metrics.analyzers.lizard_complexity` raise `ModuleNotFoundError` at test time.
CI fails on the import step of the unit test before any assertion runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/metrics/analyzers/__init__.py (new — package init, even if empty)` to the 'Files likely touched' list.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
